summary: Matched the Temple Emblem stage file by replacing asm/melee/gr/grtemblem.s with src/melee/gr/grtemblem.c and adding a matching grtemblem.h. The PR moved grtemblem from assembly to C in obj_files.mk, reconstructed its StageCallbacks and StageData, and implemented the stage init, object creation, animation, update, and collision/helper callbacks using existing ground, anime, display, zako generator, lb, OSReport, and HSD GObj APIs.
what_changed: Deleted the 282-line assembly implementation at asm/melee/gr/grtemblem.s.
Added a 174-line C implementation at src/melee/gr/grtemblem.c.
Added src/melee/gr/grtemblem.h with static prototypes for all local grTRoy_* functions used by the file.
Updated obj_files.mk so grtemblem builds from $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/gr/grtemblem.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/gr/grtemblem.s.o.
Recreated grTFe_803E97C0 as a StageCallbacks table with callback groups for three stage gobj IDs, including the bitfield value expressed as (1 << 30) | (1 << 31).
Recreated grTFe_803E981C as a StageData object with stage ID 64, data file path "/GrTFe.dat", init/load/exit callbacks, item/position callbacks, and final flag value 1.

Implemented stage startup grTRoy_802243F8 by setting stage_info.unk8C.b4 to 0 and b5 to 1, spawning gobj IDs 0, 1, and 2 through grTRoy_80224498, then calling Ground_801C39C0, Ground_801C3BB4, Ground_801C4210, and Ground_801C42AC.

Implemented grTRoy_80224498 as the shared stage gobj setup helper: fetch Ground_801C14D0(gobj_id), clear Ground x8_callback and xC_callback, call GObj_SetupGXLink with grDisplay_801C5DB0, install callback3 into x1C_callback when present, run callback0 when present, and install callback2 as an HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 process when present.
Preserved the failure path in grTRoy_80224498 by calling OSReport with "%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)\n", file name "grtemblem.c", line 0xC3, and the gobj ID.
Implemented animation setup callbacks using grAnime_801C8138 and Ground map_id from gobj->user_data or GET_GROUND(gobj).

Implemented the two similar map-object callbacks grTRoy_802245BC and grTRoy_8022464C using Ground_801C2ED0(gobj->hsd_obj, gp->map_id) followed by grAnime_801C8138(gobj, gp->map_id, false), with MUST_MATCH-only dummy stack padding.

Implemented simple stubs and boolean callbacks that return false or true according to the deleted assembly: several no-op callbacks, false-returning update checks, grTRoy_802246C8 returning false, and grTRoy_802246D0 returning true.
Implemented grTRoy_8022446C to call grZakoGenerator_801CAE04(0), grTRoy_80224614 to call lb_800115F4 then Ground_801C2FE0, and grTRoy_802246A4 to call Ground_801C2FE0.
smart_moves: Used existing project stage abstractions, especially StageCallbacks and StageData, instead of keeping raw .4byte data tables from assembly.
Translated stage_info byte bit manipulation into named bitfield assignments stage_info.unk8C.b4 and stage_info.unk8C.b5, making the intent clearer while matching the emitted code.
Centralized object setup behavior in grTRoy_80224498 and indexed directly into grTFe_803E97C0 by gobj_id, mirroring the assembly table stride while keeping the C idiom simple.
Preserved nullable callback behavior exactly: callback3 is installed only when non-null, callback0 is invoked only when non-null, and callback2 is scheduled only when non-null.
Retained the original diagnostic string and source-line value for the OSReport failure path, which is often required for matching data/string layout.
Used MUST_MATCH-only local padding arrays in functions where the stack frame needed to match without changing normal semantics.
Expressed the 0xC0000000 callback flag as (1 << 30) | (1 << 31), documenting the bit composition while keeping the same value.
decomp_lessons: For stage files, the asm data section often maps cleanly to StageCallbacks arrays plus a StageData initializer; reconstructing those tables first helps drive function signatures.

Ground gobj setup code commonly follows a reusable pattern: obtain the gobj by ID, access Ground via user_data, clear callbacks, set up GX link with grDisplay_801C5DB0, then attach table-driven callbacks/processes.
Bitfield accesses on stage_info can replace rlwimi/lbz/stb sequences when the relevant struct fields are known.
Preserving OSReport strings, file names, and line numbers can matter for matching, especially when those strings were embedded after stage data.
MUST_MATCH dummy stack padding can be isolated behind preprocessor guards to satisfy stack layout without affecting non-matching builds.
Repeated stage object callback bodies can often identify a shared stage pattern: call Ground_801C2ED0 on gobj->hsd_obj with Ground map_id, then start grAnime_801C8138 with the same map_id.
Even trivial no-op and constant-return callbacks should be declared and implemented with signatures matching their StageData or StageCallbacks slots, because callback table typing drives data layout.
naming_conventions: The matched file keeps address-based symbol names with the existing stage prefix grTRoy_* even though the file is grtemblem.c and data symbols are grTFe_*.
Stage data symbols use the existing address-suffixed naming convention: grTFe_803E97C0 for the callback table and grTFe_803E981C for the StageData object.
Local static functions are declared in grtemblem.h with their address-based names and argument types.
The header guard uses the project-local lowercase form _grtemblem_h_.
Ground pointer locals use the short project convention gp, and callback-table locals use cb.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the assembly object from the build only after adding the matching C object in obj_files.mk.
Translated assembly table data into typed C initializers for StageCallbacks and StageData while preserving ordering, null slots, string path, and flag values.
Matched stage_info bit writes using named bitfields instead of manual byte masks.
Used callback null checks to reproduce the branch structure around callback3 assignment, callback0 invocation, and callback2 process creation.
Kept grTRoy_802245BC and grTRoy_8022464C stack behavior with `#ifdef MUST_MATCH u8 _[8]; #endif`.
Preserved constant return values from leaf assembly functions as simple C returns: false for several bool callbacks and true for grTRoy_802246D0.
Kept OSReport arguments equivalent to the assembly, including line constant 0xC3.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR had one review but no review body or feedback excerpt was included, so reviewer intent cannot be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Find other trophy/stage files with StageCallbacks tables that can be converted from asm to typed C initializers.
Search for grTFe_803E97C0 or StageData grTFe_803E981C consumers to confirm naming and linkage expectations.
Compare grtemblem.c against neighboring matched stage files for common callback helper patterns around Ground_801C14D0 and GObj_SetupGXLink.
Search for other functions requiring MUST_MATCH dummy stack padding around Ground_801C2ED0 plus grAnime_801C8138.
Investigate whether grTRoy_* names in grtemblem should eventually receive semantic names once enough related stage code is understood.
